cases.description: A range of numbers is coerced to logical values (0=FALSE, nonzero=TRUE); one zero makes the whole AND FALSE
cases.expected_note: Numeric coercion in ranges confirmed via https://www.ablebits.com/office-addins-blog/excel-and-or-xor-not-functions/ since Microsoft's own AND page doesn't give an explicit numeric example.

cases.description: A range of all-nonzero numbers coerces to all-TRUE -> TRUE
An arbitrary text literal (not a range) does not evaluate to a logical value -> #VALUE!, per doc: 'arguments must evaluate to logical values'
